name: always_free
code:
proof fn phi_1_always_free(os: &OS, target_proc_ptr: ProcPtr, va_range: &VaRange4K)
    requires
        os.wf(),
        os.proc_dom().contains(target_proc_ptr),
        va_range.wf(),
    ensures
        os.address_space_range_free(target_proc_ptr, va_range) == true,
{
}
reason: If provable, the spec vacuously treats every VA range as free, making the check useless and allowing double-mapping

name: never_free_nonempty
code:
proof fn phi_2_never_free_nonempty(os: &OS, target_proc_ptr: ProcPtr, va_range: &VaRange4K)
    requires
        os.wf(),
        os.proc_dom().contains(target_proc_ptr),
        va_range.wf(),
        va_range.len > 0,
    ensures
        os.address_space_range_free(target_proc_ptr, va_range) == false,
{
}
reason: If provable, the spec says no non-empty range is ever free, blocking all new mappings even in a pristine address space

name: proc_independent_result
code:
proof fn phi_3_proc_independent_result(os: &OS, p1: ProcPtr, p2: ProcPtr, va_range: &VaRange4K)
    requires
        os.wf(),
        os.proc_dom().contains(p1),
        os.proc_dom().contains(p2),
        p1 != p2,
        va_range.wf(),
    ensures
        os.address_space_range_free(p1, va_range) == os.address_space_range_free(p2, va_range),
{
}
reason: If provable, different processes always agree on which ranges are free, violating address-space isolation

name: free_but_first_va_mapped
code:
proof fn phi_4_free_but_first_va_mapped(os: &OS, target_proc_ptr: ProcPtr, va_range: &VaRange4K)
    requires
        os.wf(),
        os.proc_dom().contains(target_proc_ptr),
        va_range.wf(),
        va_range.len > 0,
        os.address_space_range_free(target_proc_ptr, va_range),
    ensures
        os.get_address_space(target_proc_ptr).dom().contains(va_range@[0]),
{
}
reason: If provable, a range declared free actually has its first VA mapped, meaning the spec's notion of free contradicts the address-space contents

name: single_page_always_free
code:
proof fn phi_5_single_page_always_free(os: &OS, target_proc_ptr: ProcPtr, va_range: &VaRange4K)
    requires
        os.wf(),
        os.proc_dom().contains(target_proc_ptr),
        va_range.wf(),
        va_range.len == 1,
    ensures
        os.address_space_range_free(target_proc_ptr, va_range) == true,
{
}
reason: If provable, single-page ranges are always free regardless of mappings, suggesting the spec has an off-by-one or vacuous quantifier for len==1
